transcription_cleaned:
Doctor: Hi, I'm your doctor. Can you tell me about your symptoms?
Patient: Yes, I had a bump on my hip and felt a lot of pain after I fell 11 months ago.
Doctor: Hmm, I see. What did the emergency room diagnose you with?
Patient: They said it was a hematoma around my hip.
Doctor: I understand. And did your symptoms worsen over time?
Patient: Yes, I had recurrent falls.
Doctor: Okay. Did you have any radiographic imaging done?
Patient: Yes, I had a CT scan without intravenous contrast done six weeks before I was diagnosed with cancer.
Doctor: And what did the CT scan show?
Patient: It confirmed that I didn't have a displaced fracture, but it indicated a tissue collection that was suspicious for a hematoma.
Doctor: I see. And then, did you have an MRI done?
Patient: Yes, I did. The MRI demonstrated a soft-tissue mass in my left hip that was suspicious for sarcoma.
Doctor: Okay, got it. And then you had a consultation with an orthopedic oncologic specialist, right?
Patient: Yes, I did. And I underwent a radical resection of the sarcoma the next day.
Doctor: Okay. I have your pathology report here. It says that you were diagnosed with a high-grade undifferentiated pleomorphic sarcoma. The margins were negative and the closest evidence of cancer was 2 mm within surgical margins.
Patient: Hmm.
Doctor: And after the surgery, you had a chest CT that showed two tiny lung nodules with the larger nodule measuring 4 mm. This indicates a recommended follow-up of three to six months.
Patient: Okay.
Doctor: Also, there was a small hypodense lesion in your spleen that was inadequately visualized.
Patient: Hmm.
Doctor: The tumor stage was established to be Stage III measuring 13.5 cm at its greatest dimension. And the TNM staging was established to be pT2a, cN0, cM0 without evidence of lymph node involvement or distant metastases.
Patient: Okay.
Doctor: You had two follow-up appointments with the surgeon, who recommended a consult to medical and radiation oncology. And you had consultations at two other institutions including a sarcoma clinic.
Patient: Yes, I did.
Doctor: And they recommended adjuvant chemotherapy for you.
Patient: Yes, they did.
Doctor: Okay, I will schedule a follow-up appointment for you. It's important that you follow the recommendations of the medical and radiation oncologists.
Patient: Yes, I will. Thank you, doctor.
clinical_summary: A 67-year-old woman presented with a rapidly enlarging left hip mass following a fall 11 months ago, initially diagnosed as a hematoma. Imaging revealed a soft-tissue mass suspicious for sarcoma, confirmed as a high-grade undifferentiated pleomorphic sarcoma post-resection. The tumor was staged as pT2a, cN0, cM0 (Stage III). Post-surgery, she received adjuvant radiation and was recommended for chemotherapy due to new pulmonary nodules suggestive of metastasis.

differential_diagnosis.rationale: Confirmed by pathology after resection of the left hip mass.
New pulmonary nodules on follow-up imaging suggest possible metastasis.
Large left hip fluid collection noted on surveillance imaging could indicate a chronic hematoma or seroma.

final_report.subjective: The patient reports a history of a left hip mass and pain following a fall 11 months ago, initially diagnosed as a hematoma. She experienced recurrent falls and underwent imaging that revealed a suspicious mass, later confirmed as a high-grade undifferentiated pleomorphic sarcoma. She reports current pain management with Percocet and acetaminophen.
final_report.objective: Physical examination reveals lymphedema of the left lower extremity and abdomen, with functional limitations in sleep, standing, dressing, and household activities. Imaging shows new pulmonary nodules and a large left hip fluid collection.
final_report.assessment: The patient has a history of high-grade undifferentiated pleomorphic sarcoma of the left hip, now with new pulmonary nodules suggestive of metastasis. She also presents with lymphedema and functional limitations likely secondary to her cancer treatment and disease progression.
final_report.plan: Continue current pain management regimen. Schedule follow-up with medical and radiation oncology for further evaluation and management of potential metastasis. Consider additional imaging to assess the nature of the left hip fluid collection. Encourage physical therapy to address functional limitations and lymphedema.
